key_insight: TREK expands a model’s exploration support with short distillation from verified teacher solutions, then uses GRPO to refine those newly reachable reasoning modes.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical way to improve hard-problem performance when on-policy RL stalls, and it works with black-box, white-box, or self-context teachers.
method: Find prompts where the student has very low pass rate.
Generate verified candidate solutions from a proposal source and keep the top-r by student likelihood.
Apply a brief forward-KL phase to pull useful modes into support, then resume standard GRPO refinement.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, TREK improves Qwen3 models on AIME 2024/2025 and boosts agentic task success, including Qwen3-8B from 36.9 to 40.3 on AIME 2025 and from 47.9 to 51.1 on AIME 2024 (avg@16).
watch_item: TREK depends on access to verified candidate trajectories, so its gains may be limited by proposal quality, verification cost, or mismatch between teacher-generated modes and the student’s target domain.
paper_title: TREK: Distill to Explore, Reinforce to Refine
